page: Amazon Web Services c4.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Xen hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2666 v3 processor with an x86_64 architecture, delivering 36 dedicated vCPUs across 18 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 2.9 GHz and includes 25 MB of L3 cache. Memory capacity is configured at 60.0 GB, yielding a resource density of 1.67 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the c4.8xlarge in the average performance tier across most compute and memory metrics. The instance achieves a single-core stress-ng CPU score of 1435.59 and a multi-core score of 34011.03. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 1893.6 GB/sec, which drops to 113.49 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, alongside a write bandwidth of 48.21 GB/sec. In application-specific tests, the server handles 30.6 million Redis SET operations per second and achieves a static web serving throughput of 23.15 GB/sec.

While the instance demonstrates solid multi-threaded capabilities, such as a bzip3 decompression speed of 131.55 GB/sec and a Geekbench multi-core score of 10598.0, it exhibits limitations in modern machine learning tasks. Large language model inference speeds are weak to poor, with text generation on a small 135M model reaching only 62.63 tokens per second, and prompt processing on a 70B model dropping to 5.02 tokens per second. Given the lack of local storage and GPU acceleration, the c4.8xlarge is best suited for CPU-bound workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, high-throughput web serving, and batch processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c4.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 36 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon E5-2666 v3 processor running at 2.9 GHz. Built on the Xen hypervisor, it provides 60.0 GB of system memory, or 1.67 GB per core, but lacks local storage and GPU hardware. Network performance is anchored by a 10 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Benchmark data shows average performance across standard compute tasks, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 10598.0 and a Redis SET throughput of 30.6 million operations per second. However, the instance exhibits weak performance in LLM text generation, making it less suitable for modern AI workloads. It is highly effective for traditional CPU-intensive tasks, including software compilation, static web serving, and multi-threaded data decompression.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c4.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 36 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 60.0 GB of RAM, and 10 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering reliable performance for CPU-bound workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c4.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 36 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 60.0 GB of RAM, and a 10 Gbps network baseline.
tagline: A dedicated 36-vCPU compute-optimized instance designed for high-throughput CPU workloads, software compilation, and multi-threaded data processing.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2666 v3 processor with 36 dedicated vCPUs and 18 physical cores.
60.0 GB of system memory providing 1.67 GB of RAM per core.
10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU.
Strong multi-threaded performance, achieving 131.55 GB/sec in bzip3 decompression.
Weak performance in large language model text generation and inference tasks.
Best suited for software compilation, ray tracing, and static web serving.
